thinking.text:
The user wants me to escalate this to the rewards team. I should transfer this to a human agent since this involves correcting rewards discrepancies that I cannot fix directly. Let me use the transfer_to_human_agents function with an appropriate reason and summary.

The reason would be something like "specialized_department_required" since this needs to go to the rewards team for correction.

tool_calls.Name: transfer_to_human_agents
tool_calls.Arguments:
{"reason": "specialized_department_required", "summary": "Customer Amara Okonkwo (user_id: 755bcb4d5d) has identified discrepancies in their credit card rewards. After reviewing all 26 transactions across their Silver Rewards Card and Business Silver Rewards Card, I found 6 transactions with incorrect rewards calculations:

**Business Silver Rewards Card (5 under-credited transactions):**
1. 03/22/2025 - JetBlue Airways ($315.00) - Earned 3,150 pts, expected 6,300 pts (short 3,150 pts)
2. 04/10/2025 - Microsoft 365 ($79.99) - Earned 160 pts, expected 1,600 pts (short 1,440 pts)
3. 08/25/2025 - Southwest Airlines ($380.00) - Earned 1,520 pts, expected 3,800 pts (short 2,280 pts)
4. 09/15/2025 - Zoom Video ($149.99) - Earned 600 pts, expected 1,500 pts (short 900 pts)
5. 10/15/2025 - Coursera ($49.99) - Earned 50 pts, expected 500 pts (short 450 pts)

**Silver Rewards Card (1 over-credited transaction):**
1. 05/18/2025 - GitHub Enterprise ($255.00) - Earned 2,550 pts, expected 1,020 pts (over 1,530 pts)

**Net underpayment:** 6,690 points ($66.90)

The Business Silver Rewards Card was opened 02/13/2025 and qualifies for the Double Cash Back Promo (20% on travel/software, 2% on other) for the first 6 months through 08/13/2025. Several travel and software transactions appear to have been credited at incorrect rates. Customer requests the rewards team review and credit the missing points to their account."}
